title: Venezuela capital hit by double earthquake as experts warn of high death toll
summary_full: Delcy Rodríguez declares state of emergency after buildings collapse across Caracas, with tremors felt in ColombiaVenezuela earthquake – latest updatesVenezuela’s interim leader, Delcy Rodríguez, has declared a state of emergency after the country was struck by two powerful earthquakes that collapsed dozens of buildings and killed at least 32 people, with experts predicting the death toll could rise significantly.Rodríguez said hundreds were injured and more casualties were expected. The two strong earthquakes hit within a minute of each other. The first had a magnitude of 7.2 and the second 7.5, making it the most powerful to strike the country since 1900, according to the US Geological Survey (USGS). Continue reading...
